title: Uragoga chaenotricha
taxonomy: Uragoga chaenotricha is classified within the family Bignoniaceae. This family of flowering plants is commonly known as the bignonia or trumpet vine family. Uragoga chaenotricha is also a part of the order Lamiales. Further, it is classified under the class Magnoliopsida and the clade Angiosperms. The kingdom Plantae includes the species Uragoga chaenotricha.

intro: Uragoga chaenotricha, also known as Uragoga, is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The health benefits of Uragoga are attributed to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to alleviate various ailments such as fever, headaches, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Uragoga include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Uragoga is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used to make tinctures and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
